title: 实现打开 Obsidian 文件夹功能 (Task 3, AC: 3)
summary: Implementation correctly fulfills AC3 requirements for opening Obsidian folder from tray menu. Code is secure (no command injection), cross-platform compatible, well-tested with 6 test cases covering edge cases (missing, empty, whitespace, non-existent paths). Error messages are in Chinese as required. Minor observation: async wrapper is purely synchronous but acceptable for Tauri command pattern.

issues.description: The async function open_obsidian_folder() just calls the sync version without any await. While this works, it's a pseudo-async pattern.
issues.recommendation: Acceptable as-is. The Tauri command pattern prefers async signatures, and the operation is quick enough to not block meaningfully.

issues.description: The spawn() result is not awaited, meaning any error from the child process itself (not the spawn) would be silent. However, the error handling captures spawn failure.
issues.recommendation: Acceptable as-is. Desktop file managers run independently; we don't need to wait for them.

issues.description: Test helper setup_test_db_with_settings() duplicates schema with setup_test_db().
issues.recommendation: Could be refactored to share code, but not critical for this implementation.

strengths: Cross-platform support using cfg attributes for Windows/macOS/Linux
Proper path validation: checks for None, empty string, and whitespace-only values
File system existence check before attempting to open
Safe command execution using Command::new().arg() - no command injection vulnerability
Comprehensive test coverage with 6 tests covering all edge cases
Error messages in Chinese consistent with project requirements
Proper Tauri command registration in generate_handler![]
Good error propagation via tray-error event for frontend notification
Consistent use of tracing for logging
Tests handle CI environment gracefully (valid path test accounts for spawn failures)
